text: The initial laparoscopic view shows the left lobe of the liver and gallbladder covered with omentum, which is adhered to the anterior abdominal wall. The surgeon introduces a suction apparatus through a port in the right hypochondrium, below the right costal margin, to separate the omental adhesions. Pus is visible as it is suctioned out, with some collected in a syringe for culture and sensitivity testing.

The surgeon visualizes the fundus of the gallbladder and attempts to separate the omentum from the gallbladder using blunt dissection through a newly created 5mm port in the epigastrium, without employing any energy sources.
The surgeon performs a thorough peritoneal lavage using eight to ten liters of normal saline, visibly irrigating the abscess cavity within the abdominal cavity.
The surgeon performs a cholecystostomy by introducing an SPC trocar to pierce the gallbladder. After removing the trocar, a 14 French Foley's tube is inserted into the gallbladder for drainage.
The surgeon performs another wash of the surgical area and inserts a 32 French drainage tube, positioning it in the sub-hepatic space.
